title: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK
summary: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK  Riot Games
content:
We’re welcoming KICK to our global roster of streaming platforms, giving players around the world more ways to watch, co-stream, and engage with our games and esports. KICK is the primary platform for many passionate esports fans, especially in LATAM, MENA, and Europe. Bringing KICK into the mix of our global platform lineup helps us better serve those communities and brings more players together around the moments, matches, and creators they care about most.

Starting with MSI 2026, KICK will carry live broadcasts for global and regional events across LoL Esports, VALORANT, and TFT (excluding China and Korea).

In addition to expanding where fans can watch, this partnership also opens up more opportunities for creators. KICK streamers will have new ways to grow their communities and connect with Riot through ourexpanding creator and partner programsacross League, VALORANT, TFT, and Wild Rift.

The viewing experience on KICK will feel familiar to players, with access to the same drops and incentives available during our global event broadcasts on other platforms. As for KICK  streamers, we’ll also apply the same moderation practices and standards creators on other platforms are held to, ensuring a safe and positive experience for all.

title: C9, FLY, and LOS invited to pres-MSI cross-regional showdown against LCK teams, hosted by SOOP
summary: C9, FLY, and LOS invited to pres-MSI cross-regional showdown against LCK teams, hosted by SOOP  Sheep Esports
content:
Your premier source for esports news, exclusive leaks, and expert analysis. Stay ahead of the game with the latest updates from League of Legends, Valorant, CS2, and Rocket League.

With just a few hours to spare before the Mid-Season Invitational, SOOP is debuting its own event, featuring American teams facing off against lower-tier Korean teams.

The Sheep Esports team is composed of multiple writers and reporters

title: Everything You Need To Know About LCP 2026 Wild Card
summary: Everything You Need To Know About LCP 2026 Wild Card  League of Legends
content:
The LCP Wild Card makes its return this year as teams across Southeast Asia once again compete for qualification into the LCP Promotion Series, for the opportunity to be promoted into the LCP!

In line with our goal of providing wider access to the LCP, the Wild Card this year will host qualifiers in a number of regions including Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Philippines, and Thailand.

In addition to the regional qualifiers, the final stage of qualification will be the Last Chance Qualifier, open to eligible teams from across Southeast Asia. Teams that participated in previous Wild Card Qualifiers, as well as teams from other eligible regions, may also enter the Last Chance Qualifier.

The winner of each regional qualifier, including the Last Chance Qualifier, will advance to the LCP Wild Card Playoffs, for a total of four qualified teams. The winner of the Playoffs will then move on to the LCP Promotion Series and compete for a spot in the LCP next year!

PARTICIPATING REGIONSRegions and their corresponding tournaments that are enrolled into the LCP Wild Card:

title: Riot Games Adds KICK To Its Streaming Platform Network For MSI 2026
summary: Riot Games Adds KICK To Its Streaming Platform Network For MSI 2026  Hotspawn
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'Riot Games Adds KICK To Its Streaming Platform Network For MSI 2026'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

Riot Games continues to refine the competitive structure, balancing the game's high skill ceiling with spectator entertainment. The professional scene is an ever-evolving battleground where even the smallest adjustment can make or break a team's championship aspirations. Every coach, analyst, and player must stay ahead of the curve to remain competitive.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'Riot Games Adds KICK To Its Streaming Platform Network For MSI 2026  Hotspawn' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: T1's League of Legends Worlds 2025 championship skins have been revealed
summary: T1's League of Legends Worlds 2025 championship skins have been revealed  GosuGamers
content:
T1's Worlds 2025 skins will arrive in July (Images: Riot Games).

Riot Games, the developer ofLeague of Legends(LoL) have finally revealedT1's 2025 LoL World Championship skins. T1 made history at Worlds 2025 afterclaiming their third straight title since 2023 through a gripping 3-2 victory overKT Rolster, securing the organisation's sixth World Championship and further extending its era of dominance in LoL esports.

The 2025 T1 Worlds skins will be available in Patch 26.14, which releases on 15 July 2026. Alongside the standard championship skins for all five players, Finals MVPLee “Gumayusi” Min-hyeongwill also receive a Mythic-tier Prestige Miss Fortune skin, making him the only player in the lineup to receive two commemorative cosmetics.

According to a report byInven Global, the collection is built around the "Ascended Warriors" concept, portraying T1 as competitors who fought their way to the summit before ascending into mythical beings.

The theme combines wing motifs, ascension imagery, and ornate details to represent T1's rise from elite competitors to legendary champions. The development team built the collection around three pillars: “Legacy, High Status, and Effortlessly Cool”, with shared visual elements appearing across all six skins.

Choi “Doran” Hyeon-joonselected Ambessa Medarda for his first World Championship skin. After lifting the Summoner's Cup, Doran asked fans on social media which champion he should choose, with Ambessa, Gragas, Camille, and several others among the options. As reported bySheep Esports, he later confirmed Ambessa as his final choice during a livestream in November 2025.

title: LoL Esports |
content:
The 2026 League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational is headed to Daejeon, South Korea! MSI is the season’s midyear proving ground, where the best teams from every region collide to test their strength on the road to Worlds. With the MSI title and additional World Championship slots on the line, every match carries real consequences. Pros lock in on a rival—an enemy region, a former teammate, a lane opponent—and every game is a measure of dominance. This is MSI: rivalry-fueled, character-driven, and unapologetically bold.

The Official Rules of the 2026 Mid-Season Invitational can be foundhere.

MSI has two stages: the Play-In Stage (June 28-July 1) and the Bracket Stage (July 3-6, July 8-12). If you’re joining us in person at the event, please check out theRiot Games event policy.

This year, the teams competing from each of the different regions are:

Team Secret Whales (TSW)Revolve Deep Cross Gaming (DCG)

The format for MSI 2026 will look very similar to MSI 2025, with the only changes occurring in the Play-In Stage. This year, Play-In Stage will feature a 4-team Double-Elimination Bracket that will qualify a single team into the Bracket Stage. The Bracket Stage will remain an 8-team Double-Elimination Bracket.
